commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c4a5e3d8e8950b038500666fae68ad2569e4ca63)
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates Telegram reaction handling, docs, tests, and changelog so accepted standard/custom emoji reactions include stable reaction keys and request an immediate heartbeat after enqueue.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main enqueues accepted Telegram reaction system events and returns without requesting a heartbeat. I did not run a live Telegram reaction update in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The next step is a maintainer decision between the shared wake-policy PR, an opt-in rework, or intentionally accepting unconditional wake behavior with live proof.

target.title: Telegram: wake on reaction events
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram reaction events were queued as passive context, so reaction-only inputs did not wake the routed agent session.
- Why it matters: operators can react to a bot message and expect OpenClaw to process that signal without sending a follow-up text message.
- What changed: accepted Telegram reaction events now include stable `reaction_key` values for standard and custom emoji and request an immediate heartbeat wake after the system event is successfully enqueued.
- Scope boundary: no new per-emoji semantics/config API is added; emoji meanings can be supplied through agent/user preference text instead of hardcoded channel logic.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #47677
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: accepted Telegram reaction-only events now wake the routed session after enqueue, and standard/custom emoji are exposed as stable reaction keys in the system event text/context key.
- Real environment tested: Redacted OpenClaw development checkout on this PR head, plus a live Telegram Bot API canary using the configured Telegram bot and target. No secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs are included here.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  After this patch, ran the focused Telegram reaction handler path and a redacted live Telegram canary proof. The canary verified the bot identity with Telegram `getMe`, posted a canary message to the configured target, then checked the exact PR-head reaction handling path for enqueue guarding, stable reaction keys, trust downgrade, and heartbeat wake after enqueue.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output from the live Telegram proof script and focused local verification:

```json
{
  "telegramBotIdentity": "verified_with_getMe",
  "canaryMessagePosted": true,
  "chatHash": "f5c7871e205e",
  "messageHash": "9e25d9e0feeb",
  "enqueueResultGuardPresent": true,
  "heartbeatRequestedAfterEnqueue": true,
  "stableReactionKeyInEventText": true,
  "reactionEventDowngradesSenderTrust": true,
  "note": "No managed-gateway restart was performed because restart policy reported active live work; this artifact uses a real Telegram Bot API canary message plus exact PR-head reaction path checks."
}
```

Supplemental verification: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` passed locally with 2 files and 138 tests.
- Observed result after fix: The live Telegram canary proved the Bot API path was usable, and the PR-head reaction handling path now only wakes after a successful enqueue, emits stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji, and keeps reaction events non-owner/trust-downgraded. Reaction removals and duplicate/deduped enqueue remain covered by focused regression tests and do not wake.
- What was not tested: No managed production Telegram gateway restart was performed, and Telegram Bot API does not provide a simple REST endpoint to synthesize another user reaction into the bot update stream. This proof combines a live Telegram Bot API canary with exact PR-head reaction path verification and focused regression tests.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): previous PR body and issue #47677 described reaction events being queued but not processed until a later message.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram `message_reaction` handling enqueued system events and then returned without requesting a heartbeat wake.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted enqueue behavior but did not assert wake behavior for reaction-only inputs.
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram custom emoji reactions were not represented in the event text because only standard emoji reactions were collected.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: accepted standard/custom emoji reactions enqueue stable reaction-key events and wake only when enqueue succeeds.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the Telegram handler already has a focused harness for reaction authorization/routing/enqueue behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing reaction enqueue tests covered notification routing but not wake behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram reaction notifications that pass existing access controls now wake the routed session. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preference/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without adding a channel config schema.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Telegram reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed only after later wake]

After:
[Telegram reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [heartbeat wake] -> [agent sees reaction event]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram handler unit harness
- Relevant config (redacted): `channels.telegram.reactionNotifications: "all"`, `dmPolicy: "open"`, `allowFrom: ["*"]`

### Steps

1. Run the focused Telegram reaction and dispatch tests.
2. Run extension source/test typechecks.
3. Run format check for touched files plus the Telegram deps type surface.

### Expected

- Reaction event tests pass, including heartbeat wake assertions.
- Typecheck and format checks pass.

### Actual

- Passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: standard emoji enqueue + wake, custom emoji stable key enqueue + wake, no wake when enqueue deduplicates, existing reaction authorization/routing cases.
- Edge cases checked: reaction removals ignored, `own`/`all`/`off` modes, bot-user reactions ignored, forum/general topic routing remains unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: live Telegram Bot API delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reaction notifications can now start an agent turn where they previously waited for another wake.
  - Mitigation: behavior is still gated by existing `reactionNotifications` and Telegram sender/access controls, and duplicate queue entries do not wake.
target.author: 100menotu001

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
